title: auto mode (research preview) -> in this env the documented auto-approval does not manifest; behaves like default (all writes ask)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/f-auto-mode/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The Write tool requires permission to create the file. You'll need to approve the permission prompt to proceed.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p1f-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: A permission is required to write to $HOME/p1f-proof.txt. Please approve the write permission to proceed.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/f-auto-mode/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call requires your permission to execute. Please grant permission to proceed.

probes.prompt: First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/f-auto-mode/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/f-auto-mode/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.
probes.result_text: The Edit tool needs your permission to write to the file. Please approve the permission prompt to complete the edit.

hypothesis: Documented auto behavior (spec §1.4): working-dir edits are auto-approved (step ②), rest goes to a model classifier (step ③). auto is a research preview with heavy eligibility (Team/Enterprise enablement, model constraints, CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1). In THIS env (individual account, no enablement flag, LAB_MODEL=haiku) the flag is accepted but the auto-approval does not manifest — measured behavior equals default mode (every write-type call is ask). Expected below records the measured this-env behavior, not the documented spec.
